content.text: Destructuring and spread/rest are ES2015 syntax features that dramatically simplify how you extract, combine, and pass data. They appear throughout Angular: in component inputs, service methods, signal updates, and RxJS operators. Understanding both saves significant boilerplate.

attrs.alt: Destructuring and spread/rest — visual reference for objects and arrays
attrs.title: Destructuring and spread/rest — visual reference for objects and arrays

content.text: Object Destructuring
Object destructuring extracts named properties from an object into variables in a single statement.

Renaming
Default values
Nested destructuring
Rest in objects
In function parameters
Array Destructuring
Array destructuring extracts elements by
position
rather than name. Any iterable (arrays, strings, Sets, Map entries, generator results) can be destructured.
Basic extraction and skipping
Default values and swapping
Iterable destructuring
Spread Operator
The spread operator (
) expands an iterable into individual elements. Used inside array literals and function calls, it distributes the items as separate values.
Spread into arrays
Spread into function arguments
Spread into objects
Rest Parameters
Rest parameters collect remaining function arguments into an array. They must be the last parameter.
Destructuring & Spread in Angular
Immutable signal updates
Destructuring in HttpClient responses
Spread in route and query params
Common Mistakes
Destructuring undefined — const { name } = undefined throws; guard first: obj?.name or const { name } = obj ?? {}
Spreading nested objects — { ...original } is shallow; nested objects still share references
Using rest before the last param — function fn(...a, b) is a SyntaxError; rest must be last
Expecting spread to deep-clone — it only copies the top level; use structuredClone for deep copies
Destructuring non-iterable as array — const [a] = 42 throws; objects are not iterable
Practical Patterns
Building a partial update utility
Extracting and forwarding props (component passthrough)
Multi-return patterns with destructuring
TypeScript and Destructuring
TypeScript infers types from destructuring automatically and lets you add type annotations where needed.
Spread in RxJS Pipelines
Spread is ubiquitous in RxJS operators inside Angular services — particularly for building state patches without mutating the source.
Summary

Destructuring extracts values from objects (by key) and arrays (by position) into local variables, supporting renaming, defaults, nesting, and rest. The spread operator expands iterables into individual elements for array literals, function calls, and object literals — enabling shallow clones and immutable updates. Rest parameters collect trailing function arguments into an array. In Angular, spread is the idiomatic way to produce new references for OnPush change detection and signal updates.
